License: arXiv.org perpetual non-exclusive license

arXiv:2312.16886v2 \[cs.CV\] 30 Dec 2023

# MobileVLM: A Fast, Strong and Open Vision Language Assistant for Mobile Devices

Xiangxiang Chu11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Limeng Qiao11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Xinyang Lin11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Shuang Xu11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Yang Yang1,313{}^{1,3}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 , 3 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Yiming Hu11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,

Fei Wei11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,    Xinyu Zhang11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,   Bo Zhang11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,   Xiaolin Wei11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT,   Chunhua Shen22{}^{2}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 2 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT

11{}^{1}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 1 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT Meituan Inc.
22{}^{2}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 2 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT Zhejiang University, China

33{}^{3}start\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT 3 end\_FLOATSUPERSCRIPT Dalian University of Technology, China

Work done as an intern at Meituan.

###### Abstract

We
present
MobileVLM, a competent multimodal vision language model (MMVLM) targeted to run on mobile devices. It is an amalgamation of a myriad of architectural designs and techniques that are mobile-oriented, which comprises a set of language models at
the
scale of 1.4B and 2.7B parameters, trained from scratch, a multimodal vision model that is pre-trained in the CLIP fashion, cross-modality interaction via an efficient projector. We evaluate MobileVLM on several typical VLM benchmarks.
Our models demonstrate
on par
performance compared
with a few much larger models. More importantly, we measure the inference speed on both a Qualcomm Snapdragon 888 CPU and an NVIDIA Jeston Orin GPU, and
we obtain
state-of-the-art performance
of
21.5 tokens and 65.3 tokens per second,
respectively. Our models
are available
at: [https://github.com/Meituan-AutoML/MobileVLM](https://github.com/Meituan-AutoML/MobileVLM)

## 1 Introduction

Large multimodal models (LMMs), especially the family of Visual Language Models (VLMs), rise as a promising research direction for building general-purpose assistants due to their substantially enhanced capability in both perception and reasoning \[ [127](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib127)\]. However, it has been challenging to connect the representations of the pre-trained large language models (LLMs) \[ [49](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib49), [131](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib131), [26](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib26), [118](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib118), [115](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib115)\] and the vision models to unveil the cross-modality properties, such as visual question answering, image captioning, visual knowledge reasoning and conversation, etc.

Remarkable performance on this task can be witnessed in GPT-4V \[ [90](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib90)\] and Gemini \[ [45](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib45)\], and the evaluation of their abilities can be found in \[ [1](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib1), [41](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib41)\]. However, very limited
technical details are available for these proprietary models. Simultaneously in the research community, a line of language tuning methods have been proposed \[ [2](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib2), [66](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib66), [30](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib30), [76](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib76), [74](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib74), [78](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib78), [5](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib5), [133](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib133), [126](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib126), [122](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib122)\]. For instance, Flamingo \[ [2](https://browse.arxiv.org/html/2312.16886v2/#bib.bib2)\] exploits visual tokens to condition the frozen language model via gated cross-attention layers.

raw.title: DXSKJB04B20220825C
raw.text:
LIFE IN CHINA
SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY DAILY | THURSDAY, AUGUST 25, 2022
4
By Staff Reporters
By Staff Reporters
Traditional Eastern Wisdom
Service Info
David G. Evans, 63, a member of
Royal Institute of Chemistry, has been
teaching in China for 26 years. British
native Evans is a Distinguished Professor
focusing on functional material systems
in layered and intercalated structures at
Beijing University of Chemical Technology. He was awarded the Chinese Government Friendship Award and the Meritorious Foreign Teacher award in 2001 and
2014 respectively for his unparalleled
contributions to China's modernization
drive.
In addition, his outstanding contribution to scientific research has earned
him the International Science and Technology Cooperation Award of the People's Republic of China in 2005.
A "Beijing floater"
Evans prefers to be called by his
Chinese name, Dai Wei, rather than his
English name. As a "Beijing floater," he
has lived and worked in Beijing for more
than two decades. He now not only
speaks fluent Chinese, but also has a
good understanding of Chinese culture.
One summer, more than 30 years
ago, the International Conference on
Coordination Chemistry held in Nanjing attracted hundreds of foreign delegates, and Evans was one of them. At
that time, China was still in its early
stage of reform and opening up, and
while many foreign experts knew little
about this eastern socialist country, Evans was shown the hospitality and
kindness of Chinese people. "Just take a
map and point with your finger where
you want to go, and many Chinese people will enthusiastically tell you how to
get there," he recalled.
Moreover, the trend that all sectors
of society, including academia, were
poised for growth also made him aware
of China's huge potential. So he made
up his mind to pay more visits to the
country. In the following ten years, he
visited China many times through various international academic exchange activities. On each visit he was always
amazed by the rapid development of
China and became more determined to
secure a closer relationship with the
country.
In 1996, Evans made a bold decision to give up his lucrative job in England and settle in China. At that time,
many of his colleagues and friends were
not supportive, but he persisted. So far,
time has proven that he made the right
choice.
An online celebrity
Another focus of Evans' work is to
popularize the science of chemistry. Science popularization implies disseminating scientific knowledge and fostering a
scientific way of thinking, especially
among young people, thereby inspiring
them to work in the science field in
the future. "When primary school students in the UK see me doing chemistry experiments, they scream with joy
when the reagents change color and explode; however, by the time they reach
high school, they are not surprised any
more. But in China, not only elementary school students, but also many high
school students and even college students are still amazed when they see
these experiments, since they have seldom gained any opportunity to witness
them happen," he said. In contrast to
British teenagers, Chinese high school
students had relatively fewer opportunities for hands- on experiments in the
learning process of natural science subjects, but experiments are precisely an
indispensable part of building a correct
perception of scientific phenomena.
So Evans devotes much of his spare
time to conducting experimental demonstrations in primary and secondary
schools across the country, and even created accounts on Tiktok and Kwai to
reach more people. At present, he has
more than 10 million followers on these
platforms, after his fun chemistry videos went viral, making him an online celebrity scientist. He insists that chemistry experiments are as fascinating as the
magic of J.K. Rowling, and has been
dubbed the "Dumbledore" of Beijing University of Chemical Technology.
A messenger of intercultural communication and education
Evans is also known as a messenger
of intercultural communication and education. His own unique experience is the
best example of successful intercultural
communication while also offering him
a comprehensive perspective to observe
society.
"Real cultural differences are not
as simple as others describe, they can
be extremely complex. So when one is
in a foreign culture and wants to be
able to seek better development, he
should have a positive attitude in mind,
and look for and explore new things
rather than linger on the old ones," said
Evans based on his own experience of
dealing with different cultures for decades.
He also believes that, "Seize the opportunity" is another defining cross-cultural communication lesson, and encourages young people to make an effort to
integrate into other cultures without being afraid to make mistakes. In today's
age of information explosion, it has become increasingly more essential to acquire the confidence and skills to actively integrate

decision.rationale: 来源内容明确提及David G. Evans，与目标人物戴文渊无关，属于人名错配，高置信删除。

raw.title: Daoyuan Chen's Homepage
raw.text:
Daoyuan Chen's Homepage
###### [Daoyuan Chen
(陈道源)](https://yxdyc.github.io/)
## Bio & Contact
About
Hi, I'm a researcher and engineer at Alibaba Tongyi Lab, working on*data intelligence for the era of large models and AI agents*.
I earned my Master's degree in Computer Application Technology in June 2019 from Peking University, advised by[Ying Shen](https://dblp.org/pid/01/8558-1.html),[Kai Lei](https://dblp.org/pid/64/9060.html), and[Yaliang Li](https://sites.google.com/site/yaliangli/).
I've published over 40 technical[papers](#research), with more than 20 as first author at top-tier venues including ICML, NeurIPS, ICLR, SIGMOD, KDD, and TPAMI. I’ve served as Area Chair for NeurIPS, ICLR, and ICML, and organized multiple KDD tutorials and Tianchi competitions focused on large models and data-centric AI.
I’ve learned a lot from the open-source community and am happy to deeply engage with below usage-driven open-source projects.
As maintainer and technical lead:
* • **[Data-Juicer](https://github.com/datajuicer/data-juicer):**
* A full-stack, multimodal data processing framework for foundation models —now evolving toward*task-aware, agent-driven data curation*.
* • [Trinity-RFT](https://github.com/modelscope/trinity-rft):
* Reinforcement fine-tuning infrastructure enabling self-evolving LLMs via high-quality trajectory feedback.
As core contributor from the official team:
* • [AgentScope](https://github.com/agentscope-ai/agentscope):
* An agent-oriented programming framework for building composable, observable LLM applications.
* • [FederatedScope](https://github.com/alibaba/FederatedScope):
* An easy-to-use federated learning (FL) platform supporting privacy-preserving on-device intelligence.
I'm interested in building simple, robust systems and algorithms for real-world machine learning (ML) applications, with a focus on
data-centric foundations and siting at the intersection of:
* • Large Language Models (LLMs)
* • Multimodal LLMs
* • Efficient / resource-aware ML
* • Data- and knowledge-driven ML
* • Human-centric ML & Human–AI interaction
I’m especially interested in rethinking*data as a dynamic, intelligent infrastructure*in the age of LLMs and agents, which co-evolves with
models and humans.
* • *Data–model co-evolution & context-aware quality*
Closing the loops: models help select, label, and synthesize data;
data reshapes model behavior through continuous feedback, adaptive curation, and task-aware
training pipelines. Quality is*contextual*— good for which task, user, or constraint?
* • *Synthetic data & data-centric agents*
Using LLMs, agents, and simulations to generate, perturb, and organize data —creating
“sandboxes” to probe generalization and robustness. Building language-powered agents that read, rewrite, route, and structure data across
logs and multimodal streams.
* • *Human-in-the-loop & privacy-aware data ecosystems*
Designing human–AI collaboration (dialogue, multimodal AIGC, personalization, RLHF/RLAIF), so users can shape data and policies over time.
Enabling on-device / FL with locally meaningful, private data under
real-world constraints.
Collaborations are welcome; we're currently hiring full-time
researchers/developers and self-motivated interns!Feel free to reach out if you’re interested:[daoyuanchen.cdy@alibaba-inc.com](mailto:daoyuanchen.cdy@alibaba-inc.com),[chendaoyuan@pku.edu.cn](mailto:chendaoyuan@pku.edu.cn)
## Selected
Technical Works
Full paper lists:[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=1GdfinUAAAAJ)and[DBLP](https://dblp.org/pid/217/4891.html)

The number in parentheses ('n') indicates the size of each subgroup; '#' indicates co-first author; '^' indicates industrial mentor to the first

Feedback-Driven Suite for Multimodal Data-Model Co-development**](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.11784)

# 🤗 Youwei Huang

_Former Research Engineer at the Institute of Intelligent Computing Technology, Suzhou, CAS._

🏠 **Home Page:** [https://www.devil.ren](https://www.devil.ren)
🤖 **LLM Avatar:** [https://chat.devil.ren](https://chat.devil.ren)

🏛️ **Current Affiliation:** Shenzhen Yuewa Innovation Technology Company
👨‍💻 **Job Title:** AI Scientist & Specialist
👨‍🔬 **Research Interests:** Large Language Models, Robotics, Web3

💼 I founded the organizations **[uniai-lab](https://github.com/uniai-lab)** and **[web3se-lab](https://github.com/web3se-lab)**, which host innovative projects such as [UniAI](https://github.com/uniai-lab/uniai), [LeChat](https://github.com/uniai-lab/lechat-pro), and [Web3SE Kit](https://github.com/web3se-lab/web3-sekit).
🔬 My current research focuses on **robotic control** using LLMs and multi-agent systems.
🌐 I also explore **Web3** and **blockchain**. If you're interested, let's collaborate on open-source projects.
🎓 I am seeking **PhD** or **RA** opportunities and am eager to co-author papers with fellow researchers.

raw.text:
Home - Dayiheng Liu
![Dayiheng Liu](https://liudayiheng.github.io//images/me_small.jpg)
### Dayiheng Liu
Alibaba DAMO Academy
Follow
* **Hangzhou, China
* [**Github](https://github.com/liudayiheng)
* [**Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=pPLQrX4AAAAJ&hl=zh-CN)
* [**ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8755-8941)
# Home
My name is**Dayiheng Liu**(刘大一恒). I am currently an Algorithm Expert in the Alibaba DAMO. I received my Ph.D. at[DICALab](http://dicalab.cn/), Sichuan University, advised by[Prof. Jiancheng Lv](https://baike.baidu.com/item/吕建成/12631172?fr=aladdin). I led an[NLP group](https://github.com/dilab-scu/Research/blob/main/NLP/README.md)of DICALab which focuses on text generation. I joined the Alibaba DAMO Academy in January 2021.
I have published over 50 papers in leading NLP/AI journals and conferences such as ACL/ICML/NeurIPS/EMNLP/AAAI/CIKM/NAACL. I have participated in some Github projects (over 5K Github stars) as a core contributor, such as[Qwen](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen)(通义千问),[PolyLM](https://github.com/DAMO-NLP-MT/PolyLM),[ProphetNet](https://github.com/microsoft/ProphetNet), and[GLGE](https://github.com/microsoft/glge). We also released some LLM models (over 1 million downloads to date) on Huggingface and Modelscope, such as[Qwen-7B](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen-7B),[Qwen-14B](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen-14B), and[PolyLM-13B](https://huggingface.co/DAMO-NLP-MT/polylm-13b).
**Interests:**Large Language Models (LLM), Natural Language Generation (NLG)
## Honors and Awards
* Ranked 1st in[C-Eval Leaderboard](https://cevalbenchmark.com/static/leaderboard.html), Sep 11, 2023
* Winner of WMT 2022 Metrics shared Tasks, 2022
* Winner of WMT 2022 QE Multilingual Tasks, 2022
* Winner of WMT 2021 Metrics shared Tasks, 2021

* Alibaba Star (Top 1%), Alibaba Group, 2021
* List of Top 100 Chinese Rising Stars in AI ([AI华人新星百强](https://xueshu.baidu.com/usercenter/index/aischolar)), 2021
* Excellent Doctoral Thesis of Sichuan University (10 PhDs/year), Sichuan University, 2020
* Ranked 1st in[Google’s Natural Questions Leaderboard](https://ai.google.com/research/NaturalQuestions/leaderboard), Nov 19, 2019
* ACM-ICPC Asian Regional Competition Silver Medal, Asian, 2015
* CCF CSP (Certified Software Professional）C/C++ 430 scores (Top 0.1%), China, 2015
* Computer Science and Technology Star (1/370+), Sichuan University, 2015
* Winner of Chengdu International Software Design Competition (Top1), China, 2015## Publications

The Deep Vision Lab (DVLab) is a top-tier research institute on computer vision and machine learning established by Prof. Jiaya Jia. raw.title: Qiyuan.Tech
raw.text:
Qiyuan.Tech
![](https://w.wallhaven.cc/full/rd/wallhaven-rdkz7m.png)
# Qiyuan.Tech
Research and development of AGI for the benefit of mankind.
## Introducing Qwen1.5
GITHUB HUGGING FACE MODELSCOPE DEMO DISCORD
Introduction In recent months, our focus has been on developing a “good” model while optimizing the developer experience. As we progress towards Qwen1.5, the next iteration in our Qwen series, this update arrives just before the Chinese New Year.
With Qwen1.5, we are open-sourcing base and chat models across six sizes: 0.5B, 1.8B, 4B, 7B, 14B, and 72B. In line with tradition, we’re also providing quantized models, including Int4 and Int8 GPTQ models, as well as AWQ and GGUF quantized models....
[](https://qiyuan-tech.github.io/blog/qwen1.5/)
## Introducing Qwen-VL
Along with the rapid development of our large language model Qwen, we leveraged Qwen’s capabilities and unified multimodal pretraining to address the limitations of multimodal models in generalization, and we opensourced multimodal model Qwen-VL in Sep. 2023. Recently, the Qwen-VL series has undergone a significant upgrade with the launch of two enhanced versions, Qwen-VL-Plus and Qwen-VL-Max. The key technical advancements in these versions include:
Substantially boost in image-related reasoning capabilities; Considerable enhancement in recognizing, extracting, and analyzing details within images and texts contained therein; Support for high-definition images with resolutions above one million pixels and images of various aspect ratios....
[](https://qiyuan-tech.github.io/blog/qwen-vl/)
## Introducing Qwen
4 months after our first release of Qwen-7B, which is the starting point of our opensource journey of large language models (LLM), we now provide an introduction to the Qwen series to give you a whole picture of our work as well as our objectives. Below are important links to our opensource projects and community.
PAPER GITHUB HUGGING FACE MODELSCOPE DISCORD
Additionally, we have WeChat groups for chatting and we invite you to join the groups through the provided link in our GitHub readme....
[](https://qiyuan-tech.github.io/blog/qwen/)
## OFA: Towards Building a One-For-All Model
2022 is a year of generalist models! With the bloom of multimodal pretraining, especially the unified model, we have witnessed the opportunity to building a generalist model that is capable of processing tasks of different modalities or multi-modalities! Thus, we propose OFA1, namely One-For-All, a unified multimodal pretrained model that unifies understanding and generation tasks concerning modalities into a single framework, and we pretrain OFA with the instruction-based multitask-pretraining that endows it with multiple capabilities....
[](https://qiyuan-tech.github.io/blog/ofa/)
## OFASys: Enabling Multitask Learning with One Line of Code!
Intro Generalist Models are hot! We all see an opportunity towards a real generalist model by multimodal multitask learning. We previously release an opensourced unified multimodal pretrained model OFA for this goal. However, we actually met a lot of difficulties in our implementation. For example, it is hard to set up multiple tasks concerning multiple modalities, and it is hard to organize multitask learning, e.g., how to batchify your data and how to make your training stable....
[](https://qiyuan-tech.github.io/blog/ofasys/)
[](#top)

decision.rationale: 来源是Qiyuan.Tech的GitHub页面，介绍Qwen1.5模型，与戴文渊无直接关联，属于错误挂载。高置信删除候选。

raw.title: 戴潍娜--专题--中国作家网
raw.text:
戴潍娜，生于江苏南通，诗人、学者。毕业于牛津大学，现为中国社会科学院外国文学研究所副研究员，出版诗集《灵魂体操》《以万物为情人》《我的降落伞坏了》等，戏剧《侵犯》《水泥玫瑰》，翻译有《天鹅绒监狱》等。主编翻译诗刊《光年》。荣获2017太平洋国际诗歌奖年度诗人、2020剑桥徐志摩银柳叶青年诗歌奖。 
 Dai Weina, a poet and scholar, was born in Nantong, Jiangsu province. She graduated from the University of Oxford and is currently an associate researcher at the Institute of Foreign Literature of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. Dai Weina has published several poetry collections, including Soul Exercises, Loving All Things, and My Parachute is Broken, as well as plays such as Invasion and Concrete Roses. She is the translator of Miklós Haraszti’s L’artiste D’etat and the editor-in-chief of Light Years, a review of translated foreign poems. She was honored as Poet of the Year at the 2017 Pacific International Poetry Awards and the received the 2020 Cambridge Xu Zhimo Silver Willow Award for Young Poets.

raw.title: Dr. Kai-Fu Lee
raw.text:
### Dr. Kai-Fu Lee

**Chairman and CEO of Sinovation Ventures and President of Sinovation Venture’s Artificial Intelligence Institute, and Founder of 01.AI.**

Dr. Kai-Fu Lee is the Chairman and CEO of [Sinovation Ventures](https://sinovationventures.com/), founder of 01.AI, and President of Sinovation Venture’s Artificial Intelligence Institute. Sinovation Ventures, managing US$2 billion dual currency investment funds, is a leading venture capital firm focusing on developing the next generation of Chinese high-tech companies.

Prior to founding Sinovation in 2009, Lee was the President of Google China, and a senior executive at Microsoft, SGI, and Apple.

Lee received his bachelor degree in computer science from Columbia University, Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon University (CMU), as well as Honorary Doctorate Degrees from both Carnegie Mellon and the City University of Hong Kong.

He is the Co-Chair of Artificial Intelligence Council for World Economic Forum Center for the Fourth Industrial Revolution, Fellow of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), Times 100 in 2013, WIRED 25 Icons , Asian Business Leader 2018 by Asia House, and followed by over 50 million audience on social media.

In the field of artificial intelligence (AI), Lee built one of the first game playing programs to defeat a world champion (1988, Othello), as well as the world’s first large-vocabulary, speaker-independent continuous speech recognition system. Lee founded Microsoft Research China, which was named as the hottest research lab by MIT Technology Review. Later renamed Microsoft Research Asia, this institute trained the great majority of AI leaders in China, including CTOs or AI heads at Baidu, Tencent, Alibaba, Lenovo, Huawei, and Haier.

While with Apple, Lee led AI projects in speech and natural language, which have been featured on Good Morning America on ABC Television and the front page of Wall Street Journal. He has authored 10 US patents, and more than 100 journal and conference papers. Altogether, Lee has been in AI research, development, and investment for more than 30 years.

His New York Times and Wall Street Journal bestselling book [AI Superpowers](https://www.aisuperpowers.com/) discusses US-China co-leadership in the age of AI as well as the greater societal impacts brought upon by the AI technology revolution.

In his second book, [AI 2041,](https://www.ai2041.com/) Lee joins forces with celebrated sci-fi novelist Chen Qiufan to imagine our world in 2041 and how it will be shaped by AI. In ten gripping short stories, set 20 years in the future, Lee and Chen introduce readers to an array of eye-opening characters and situations.

### About Kai-Fu Lee

Dr Kai-Fu Lee is Chairman and CEO of Sinovation Ventures. Dr Lee is also President of Sinovation’s Artificial Intelligence Institute. Prior to founding Sinovation Ventures in 2009, Dr Lee was President of Google China. Previously, he held executive positions at Microsoft, SGI, and Apple. He was selected as one of the 100 most influential people in the world by Time Magazine in 2013.

Dr Lee has a PhD in artificial intelligence in 1988 from Carnegie Mellon University, and honorary PhD from Carnegie Mellon University and City University of Hong Kong. He is the author of seven top-selling books in Chinese, and has over fifty million followers in social media.

手搓第一个AI程序后，这位90后博士决定“颠覆式”创业
2025/12/31 10:59半岛网
****
“让个性化定制真正实现人人触手可及，这是我创业以来始终不变的目标。”在深圳前海一间摆满各类定制产品的办公室里，90后创业者徐豪说起创业初心，眼里闪着光。
2020年，还在香港中文大学读计算机图形学博士的徐豪，与四名队友一起“手搓”出一款AI图像生成产品：用户上传照片就能自动生成个性化方脸积木人偶，这个项目在创新工场DeeCamp 2020中斩获全球总冠军，担任导师的李开复评价其为“几乎是可以拿投资的商业项目”。以此为起点，徐豪创立了“方仔照相馆”品牌，迈出创业的第一步。
![](https://jcdn.xhby.net/dams-res/editing/image/202512/31/69549066e4b08c7965f6ab2c.png?image_process=resize,w_800/format,jpg)
用了五年时间，方仔照相馆从最初的一单单手搓，做到了平均12小时发货、自动化程度98%的千人千面的积木人偶设计与生产。品牌也升级到了更面向全球化的名称（UNICUS，寓意独一无二）。希望在海外市场、品类与工艺扩展、线下用户体验上创造更大的可玩性。而这份卓越成长的背后，藏着一段差点让公司止步的创业弯路。
徐豪坦言：“初期不懂‘颜值即正义’，差点把公司做死。我们花了两年才明白，技术再牛，用户不喜欢也是白搭。”他的桌面上摆着一个圆脸积木人偶——这是“方仔照相馆”的初代爆款，也是他从“技术执念”走向“以用户体验为中心”的见证。技术执念下的觉醒，一次价值千金的认知跃迁用AI改造传统积木，让用户照片变成个性化人偶，这条突破传统的道路，从一开始就布满暗礁。作为无经验、无资源、无前车之鉴的“三无”创业者，徐豪这位技术码农一头扎进了“降本增效”的经验误区。
面对个性化孤品在设计、生产、质检等环节的高昂人力成本，他带着技术派的执拗，把公司80%的资源砸进生成算法优化、工厂搭建和供应链打造，团队里开发人员与市场人员的比例一度逼近5:1。靠着这股蛮劲，他硬是将单件积木人偶的定制成本降低了75%，交付周期从15天压缩到7天。技术层面成效斐然，但市场反馈却泼了冷水：用户并不为单纯的效率提升买单，产品销量如冰封湖面，近乎纹丝不动。
资金链持续紧绷，叠加疫情后的经济寒冬，公司很快被逼到生死边缘。“按传统方式做，得养上百个积木产品设计师和质检工，成本根本扛不住。”徐豪苦笑着复盘，“我们钻入了技术优化的牛角尖，过度追求降本增效，踩了雷区。等反应过来时，公司已经快撑不下去了，只能抛开技术执念，重新琢磨用户真正需要什么。”团队紧急启动用户调研，两个致命的产品缺陷迅速浮出水面：方脸造型不符合东方审美偏好，生硬的线条缺失亲和力；包装简陋无美感，对于主打情感送礼的产品而言，无异于“自断后路”。痛定思痛后，他们果断暂缓了算力技术迭代，集中精力进行产品与包装升级，马不停蹄地与时间赛跑。一代产品的方脸被改成圆润讨喜的圆脸，包装则注入潮流设计与情感符号；同时提高了市场营销投入，在小红书、抖音等社交媒体密集推广。终于，二代产品一经推出便一举打破僵局，收获市场的热烈反响，销量急速暴涨，公司成功实现了生死逆转。![](https://jcdn.xhby.net/dams-res/editing/image/202512/31/69549131e4b00c3295589c90.png?image_process=resize,w_800/format,jpg)
经此一役，徐豪及其团队完成了对消费级AI的关键认知跃迁：用户喜爱永远是第一位，其次才是价格、产能和交付速度。技术要像舞台灯光，默默照亮情感价值的主角——这才是消费级AI的终局。
此后，他们彻底调整经营策略，摒弃盲目研发的模式，建立起长期的市场分析与用户调研机制：通过捕捉市场趋势、倾听用户心声，精准指引技术优化的方向与侧重。贴合需求的技术迭代，又进一步推动产能、交付速度迈向新台阶，形成“用户需求→技术优化→体验升级”的正向循环。AI重构行业全链条，五年沉淀筑起品牌护城河
走进UNICUS的AI设计中心，工位上坐着数十位负责系统开发的工程师和产品经理，办公室背景墙挂着一面大屏幕，实时播放着东莞工厂的生产线和质检线工况——这是徐豪花了5年打造的品牌护城河：AI艺术绘制 + CAM个性化制造全链条创新模式。
传统定制工业链条中，CAD（Computer Aided Design，计算机辅助设计）、CAE（Computer Aided Engineering，计算机辅助工程）、CAM（Computer Aided Manufacturing，计算机辅助制造）如同三个聋哑人的协作：设计师要人工设计模型，通过软件翻译成机器能理解的“语言”，工厂需耗费时间校准墨水配比、调试材质色差等，最终变为可生产的标品。而UNICUS是定制行业内少见能实现“设计－分析－生产”完整闭环的公司，通过对产品设计、测试、分拣、印刷、质检、包装等全流程进行重新定义，赋予每个环节高度的柔性化，用AI替代人工翻译环节，将创意直接转化为机器可执行的物理指令，实现高效设计与生产——6000+件千人千面产品的日产能、平均12小时发货的交付时效，让定制更快、更好，且所见即所得。
![](https://jcdn.xhby.net/dams-res/editing/image/202512/31/69549131e4b082763bcfd23e.png?image_process=resize,w_800/format,jpg)
全链条的重构，让UNICUS跳出了“礼品定制”的低频竞争赛道，成功切入“悦己定制”的高频场景。用户能随时进行定制创作，例如拍了一张爱宠照，可以即时上传定制周边。若想定制一个复杂并充满爱的场景，也能自由修改设计，即时看到效果，无需经历传统定制的繁琐流程。随着用户使用次数越来越多，AI系统会持续沉淀用户偏好，越来越懂他，从而不断优化定制体验，既筑牢了用户粘性，更形成了独特的数据壁垒。
对于其他企业而言，如果想要复制同样的模式需要投入大量的资金和时间，光生产和质检的智能化就已困难重重，更遑论打造适配不同产品工艺、从设计到生产的全链条AI系统。这意味着，未来较长的一段时间内，UNICUS 将凭借不可替代的创新优势，在个性化定制赛道持续领跑，实现稳健增长。创业以来的心得与2035蓝图
“创业这五年，最深刻的领悟是要掌好船舵、找好平衡，才能在未知和波动里寻找答案。”5年的试错与坚守，带给徐豪两条足以刻进骨子里的心得。
第一条是直面不确定性的勇气。从最初死磕算法到遭遇产品滞销，从资金链濒临断裂到成功逆转，他的每一次重大决策都如同在迷雾中探路。团队初创时，没人能预见AI定制赛道的未来，工厂老板们更是将“单件定制”视作天方夜谭，最后只能自建工厂。创业路上没有标准答案，只能靠“试错－复盘－调整”的循环艰难前行，而驱动这个循环的内核是高压下依然保持韧性、快速切换思路的能力。
第二条是动态平衡的智慧。这个世界没有真相，只有视角。商业从来不是非黑即白，没有绝对的对错。技术投入与用户体验要平衡，成本控制与品质打磨要平衡，短期盈利与长期布局更要平衡。早期他们把技术当全部，后来又学会在用户喜好与商业可持续之间找平衡点，这种平衡的前提是内部价值观高度一致。徐豪说：“我们不围绕表面的KPI努力，而是奔着用创新为他人、企业和社会带来有意义的改变，为世界留下独特价值。”
提及未来十年的规划，徐豪称，未来将引入更丰富的制造工艺、更沉浸式的交互、更好玩的IP形象，为用户呈现更加丰富而生动的人物与场景，真正实

target.objectLabel: 手搓第一个AI程序后，这位90后博士决定“颠覆式”创业

decision.rationale: 标题提及‘90后博士’，与李开复（1961年生）明显不符，属于高置信度的错误人物归属（wrong_person），符合安全自动删除条件。

[![From A Volunteer To A Leader](https://womenofcourage.asia/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/Awaken-the-courage-in-you.jpg)](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/from-a-volunteer-to-a-leader/)

by [Lilian Ong](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/author/lilian-ong/) \| Jan 28, 2021 \| [Reflections](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/category/reflections/)

I have been attending Women of Courage Asia since June 2020. This platform was introduced to me by my dearest friend, Carol Gockel. I also realised that two other friends of mine, Lynette Har and Jasmyn Yee-Ang, have also been volunteering in this community. Hence, my...[![Reflections – NOVEMBER MEM](https://womenofcourage.asia/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/lilian.jpeg)](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/2044-2/)

by [Lilian Ong](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/author/lilian-ong/) \| Dec 14, 2020 \| [Reflections](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/category/reflections/)

The Monthly Empowerment Meeting on the 24 November was our last session for 2020! It was such a heart-warming session as we never fail to share our takeaways and learn from each other! 2020 has been a very challenging year as the whole world plummeted into a Pandemic...[![Reflections – OCTOBER MEM](https://womenofcourage.asia/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/julia.jpeg)](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/reflections-october-mem/)

When we arrange for speakers for our Monthly Empowerment Meeting (MEM), we only have two requirements from them – vulnerability & courage. Our speaker for the Expert Sharing Segment didn’t need to share her fears and struggles but she did. Vivien Yap, a...[![Reflections – SEPTEMBER MEM](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/LisaChong-1200x600-1-1080x600.jpeg)](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/reflections-september-mem/)

A first for everything We have concluded another eventful Monthly Empowerment Meeting on 29 September 2020. Did you know that all of us at Women of Courage Asia have been longing for this day to come? And when it came, we were met with so many interesting challenges....[![Reflections – JULY MEM](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/Nic-1200x600-1-1080x600.jpeg)](https://www.womenofcourage.asia/reflections-july-mem/)

* 03 December 2024# How close is AI to human-level intelligence?
Large language models such as OpenAI’s o1 have electrified the debate over achieving artificial general intelligence, or AGI. But they are unlikely to reach this milestone on their own.
By
* [Anil Ananthaswamy](#author-0)[0](#Aff0)
1. Anil Ananthaswamy
1. Anil Ananthaswamy is a freelance science writer based in California.

![Abstract illustration showing a brain being formed from data surrounded by representations of 3D mapping and geospatial data.](https://media.nature.com/w767/magazine-assets/d41586-024-03905-1/d41586-024-03905-1_50265442.jpg)
Illustration: Petra Péterffy
[Access through your institution](https://wayf.springernature.com?redirect_uri=https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-03905-1)
[Buy or subscribe](#access-options)
OpenAI’s latest artificial intelligence (AI) system dropped in September with a bold promise. The company behind the chatbot ChatGPT[showcased o1](https://openai.com/index/introducing-openai-o1-preview/)— its latest suite of large language models (LLMs) —as having a “new level of AI capability”. OpenAI, which is based in San Francisco, California, claims that o1 works in a way that is[closer to how a person thinks](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-024-03169-9)than do previous LLMs.
